perturbation.original_text: Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Purpose”). Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, except as such disclosure is expressly permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Evaluation”). Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, except as such disclosure is expressly permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Purpose' to 'Evaluation' in the first sentence contradicts the defined term 'Purpose' later used in Section 2. This creates confusion about whether the 'Recipient' is restricted to only discussions related to establishing a 'mutually beneficial business relationship' or if any kind of 'Evaluation' is now accepted, introducing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will not use any Confidential Information except to the extent necessary for the internal purpose of discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship (“Evaluation”). Recipient will not disseminate or disclose any Confidential Information to any person, firm, business or governmental agency or department, except as such disclosure is expressly permitted in this Agreement. Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care. Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose and who are legally bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to Recipient under this Agreement. Neither party is obligated under this Agreement to purchase from or provide to the other party any service or product. Recipient agrees not to copy or reverse engineer, or attempt to derive the composition or underlying information, structure or ideas of any Confidential Information. No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement; Recipient does not acquire any right in or to the Confidential Information except the limited right to use it for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Purpose' to 'Evaluation' in the first sentence of the modified text creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which continues to use and define 'Purpose' as 'discussing, analyzing, and exchanging information about each party’s products, services and other offerings to determine whether the parties may enter into a mutually beneficial business relationship.' The contradicted text specifically states 'Recipient does not acquire any right in or to the Confidential Information except the limited right to use it for the Purpose'. By changing the initial definition to 'Evaluation,' the scope of permissible use of Confidential Information is altered, creating ambiguity and directly conflicting with the later, repeated references to the original definition of 'Purpose'. The Recipient's employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose' now refers to a non-existent term in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement will be and will remain the property of the Discloser; all such information in tangible form will be returned to Discloser promptly upon written request or upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and will not thereafter be retained in any form by Recipient or its Affiliates or any of their employees.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Information disclosed under this Agreement will be and will remain the property of the Discloser; all such information in tangible form will be returned to Discloser promptly upon written request or upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and will not thereafter be retained in any form by Recipient or its Affiliates or any of their employees.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' creates ambiguity. The contract defines 'Confidential Information', but not 'Proprietary Information'. It is not specified whether the two terms are interchangeable, raising a doubt about whether only Confidential Information, as defined in Section 1, needs to be returned, thus introducing an in-text contradiction in relation to the defined 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Information disclosed under this Agreement will be and will remain the property of the Discloser; all such information in tangible form will be returned to Discloser promptly upon written request or upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and will not thereafter be retained in any form by Recipient or its Affiliates or any of their employees. Except upon mutual written agreement or as may be required by law, neither party will disclose to others the existence or terms of this Agreement, the discussions that gave rise to this Agreement or the fact that there have been, or will be, discussions or negotiations covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' creates a contradiction because the contract defines 'Confidential Information' but not 'Proprietary Information'. The contradicted text uses 'Proprietary Information', and because the contract now uses a non-defined term where it previously used a defined term ('Confidential Information'), it introduces ambiguity and therefore an in-text contradiction, as it is unclear whether the obligations associated with 'Confidential Information' also apply to 'Proprietary Information' when it is not defined.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that the Protected Data is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data,' a term not defined in the agreement, creates ambiguity. Section 8 refers to the Confidential Information but now introduces Protected Data, making unclear whether breaching the Confidential Information is what causes the irreperable damage, or if anything else can cause the same irreperable damage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that the Protected Data is unique and valuable and a breach by Recipient of this Agreement will cause irreparable and continuing damage to Discloser for which money damages are insufficient. In the event of a breach or threatened breach of the obligations under this Agreement, Discloser will be entitled to: (1) seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting a bond, and (ii) indemnification from any loss or harm, including recovery of attorney’s fees, in connection with any breach or enforcement of Recipient’s obligations hereunder or the unauthorized use or release of such Confidential Information. Any such relief will be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages. Recipient must notify Discloser in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any such unauthorized release or other breach of which it is aware and will assist Discloser in remedying the unauthorized use or disclosure of Discloser’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is used consistently in the contradicted text (Section 8), specifically mentioning 'unauthorized use or disclosure of Discloser’s Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data', a term not previously defined or used consistently within the provided excerpt of Section 8, creating ambiguity and potentially conflicting obligations. The contradicted text only discusses remedies for breaches related to 'Confidential Information', not 'Protected Data'. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the remedies for breach only apply to the Confidential Information or also apply to the Protected data, making the obligations and liabilities unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Confidential Information with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Sensitive Data with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' introduces ambiguity and potential contradiction. The contract only defines 'Confidential Information', but not 'Sensitive Data'. This creates uncertainty about whether 'Sensitive Data' falls under the definition of 'Confidential Information' or comprises other data that requires protection. The contradiction occurs as a result of this change because now, Confidential information is being mixed with Sensitive Data which could mean that the term has a different meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will treat all of Discloser’s Sensitive Data with the same degree of care as Recipient treats its own Confidential Information, but not less than reasonable care. Recipient will disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to those of Recipient’s employees, consultants, and contractors who have a “need to know” the information to assist Recipient with respect to the Purpose and who are legally bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to Recipient under this Agreement. Neither party is obligated under this Agreement to purchase from or provide to the other party any service or product. Recipient agrees not to copy or reverse engineer, or attempt to derive the composition or underlying information, structure or ideas of any Confidential Information. No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement; Recipient does not acquire any right in or to the Confidential Information except the limited right to use it for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' introduces ambiguity because the contract only defines 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text then uses the undefined 'Sensitive Data' which creates a contradiction with the terms defined in the agreement. Because 'Sensitive Data' is not defined, it's unclear if the obligations regarding 'Confidential Information' also apply to 'Sensitive Data', leading to a direct conflict and uncertainty about enforcement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, indicating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will not apply to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information that Recipient can document: (a) was in the public domain at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by Discloser through no fault of Recipient; (b) was rightfully in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by the Discloser; (c) was developed by employees or agents of Recipient independently of and without reference to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information; or (d) was communicated by Discloser to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will not apply to any of Discloser’s Trade Secrets that Recipient can document: (a) was in the public domain at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by Discloser through no fault of Recipient; (b) was rightfully in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by the Discloser; (c) was developed by employees or agents of Recipient independently of and without reference to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information; or (d) was communicated by Discloser to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Trade Secrets' in Section 3 creates an inconsistency. While 'Confidential Information' is defined, 'Trade Secrets' is not, raising a question about whether these terms are equivalent. This creates an in-text contradiction as the 'Confidential Information' exception applies to trade secrets, but the 'Confidential Information' is still be referred in other clauses such as clauses (a), (c) and (d), which does not make sense and cause confusion for the reader.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 will not apply to any of Discloser’s Trade Secrets that Recipient can document: (a) was in the public domain at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by Discloser through no fault of Recipient; (b) was rightfully in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at or subsequent to the time the Confidential Information was communicated to Recipient by the Discloser; (c) was developed by employees or agents of Recipient independently of and without reference to any of Discloser’s Confidential Information; or (d) was communicated by Discloser to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence. A disclosure by Recipient of any of Discloser’s Confidential Information (a) in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body; (b) as otherwise required by law; or (c) necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement will not be considered to be a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; provided, however, that Recipient provides prompt prior written notice to the Discloser to enable Discloser to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Trade Secrets' in Section 3, while clauses (a), (c), and (d) still refer to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed Text and also contains clauses (a), (c), and (d) still refer to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because the exception initially applied to 'Confidential Information' now applies to 'Trade Secrets', but subsequent clauses still mention 'Confidential Information'. This misalignment in terminology creates an in-text contradiction according to the definition given above, since 'Trade Secret' is never clearly defined and there is no clarification as to whether 'Trade Secret' and 'Confidential Information' can be used interchangeably. Therefore, this leads to an inconsistency and confusion.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement (a) is the complete agreement of the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements whether oral or written with respect to disclosures concerning such subject matter; (b) may not be amended or modified except by in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties; and (c) will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware without regard to conflict of laws provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract (a) is the complete agreement of the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements whether oral or written with respect to disclosures concerning such subject matter; (b) may not be amended or modified except by in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties; and (c) will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware without regard to conflict of laws provisions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in Section 10(a) creates inconsistency and potential contradictions. The document is consistently referred to as 'Agreement' throughout except in this one instance. Now, the question is whether the entire text refers to an agreement or a contract, and whether those two words, have different meanings for the purpose of the definition of this specific text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract (a) is the complete agreement of the parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements whether oral or written with respect to disclosures concerning such subject matter; (b) may not be amended or modified except by in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties; and (c) will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware without regard to conflict of laws provisions. Each of the parties irrevocably consents to the exclusive personal jurisdiction of the federal and state courts located in New Castle County, Delaware as applicable, for any matter arising out of or relating to this Agreement, except that in actions seeking to enforce any order or any judgment of the federal or state courts located in New Castle County, Delaware such personal jurisdiction will be non-exclusive. Additionally, notwithstanding anything in the foregoing to the contrary, a claim for equitable relief arising out of or related to this Agreement may be brought in any court of competent jurisdiction. Neither party may assign any of its rights or obligations hereunder, except to an Affiliate, without the prior written consent of the other party. No failure or delay in exercising or any partial exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder will operate as a waiver thereof. If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remainder will be enforced as fully as possible and the unenforceable provision will be deemed modified to the limited extent required to permit its enforcement in a manner most closely representing the intention of the parties as expressed herein.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' creates inconsistency. The contradicted text also uses the term 'Agreement'. While the change itself seems minor, the explanation highlights that the terms may have different meanings for the purpose of the text. The changed text modifies 'Agreement' to 'Contract', creating a contradiction with the contradicted text, which refers to 'Agreement'. This misalignment of terminology, as defined, leads to an in-text contradiction because the document's identity (agreement vs. contract) becomes ambiguous.
